paragraphs.zh: 所以到今天为止，这类购买主要还是以 commodities（标准化商品）为主。类似地，我也试过用 agent（代理）来预订整个暑期旅行，但对于一个四口之家在航班、酒店、交通和行程安排上的选择，我还没有满意到愿意一键直接下单的程度。不过 models（模型）会变得更好，interfaces（界面）会变得更好，experiences（体验）也会变得更好。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: We're pretty agnostic to those.

Like we trust that they will evolve in a bunch of different and interesting ways and sort of the the primitives that are needed underneath the ability to expose your catalog, the shared payment token, the fraud protections are pretty agnostic to those experiences.
And so that's where we're hyper focused for merchants.

paragraphs.zh: 对这些我们基本上是比较 agnostic（不预设特定路径）的。我们相信它们会以许多不同且有意思的方式演进；而在底层真正需要的那些 primitives（基础能力），比如开放你的 catalog（商品目录）的能力、共享的 payment token（支付 token）、fraud protections（欺诈防护），对于这些不同体验其实都是比较 agnostic 的。所以，这也是我们目前为 merchants（商家）高度聚焦的方向。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 2

sentences.text: Give me an example of one of these commodities and also what the order of magnitude we're talking about when we say it's relatively small.

paragraphs.zh: 给我举一个这类 commodities（标准化商品）的例子吧，另外，当我们说“相对较小”的时候，量级大概是什么样？

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: An example of a commodity would be, like, a Halloween costume.

paragraphs.zh: 一个 commodity（标准化商品）的例子，比如说，Halloween costume（万圣节服装）。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 2

sentences.text: Got it.
Agents are buying Halloween costumes for themselves?

paragraphs.zh: 明白了。Agents（代理）是在给它们自己买 Halloween costumes（万圣节服装）吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: Agents are buying Halloween costumes.

paragraphs.zh: Agents 正在购买万圣节服装。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 2

sentences.text: That's really

paragraphs.zh: 这真的是——

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: How many lazy parents there are in the world?
Mean, I I think the consumer side is interesting too, right?
Because we talked about what do businesses need, right?
They need a they need a fast, easy way to safely expose their products, their prices, their inventory, their checkouts, understand fraud and be in control of the relationship.
From the consumer angle, the question's a little different, right?

paragraphs.zh: 世界上有多少偷懒的家长？我是说，我觉得消费者这一侧也很有意思，对吧？因为我们刚才讨论的是企业需要什么，对吧？他们需要一种快速、简单且安全的方式，来对外开放他们的产品、价格、库存和结账流程，理解欺诈风险，并且掌控这种关系。从消费者的角度看，问题就有点不一样了，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: Like, even if I'm a lazy parent, I'm not so lazy that I'm willing to give someone my payment credentials and, you know, let it rip.
So, like, the question for me is how do I safely let an agent buy on my behalf?
And have you heard of LINK?

paragraphs.zh: 比如说，就算我是个偷懒的家长，我也没懒到愿意把我的支付凭证交给别人，然后你知道的，随它去刷。所以，对我来说，问题是：我怎样才能安全地让一个 agent 代我购买？你听说过 LINK 吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 2

sentences.text: Yeah.
I've used LINK.

paragraphs.zh: 听说过。我用过 LINK。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: Okay.
Amazing.
So LINK is our consumer wallet.
What did you use it for?
Do you remember the first thing you used?

paragraphs.zh: 好的。太棒了。所以 LINK 是我们的消费者钱包。你当时用它做了什么？你还记得你第一次用它是做什么吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 2

sentences.text: I mean, I use it all the time.
So it's, like, everywhere.

paragraphs.zh: 我的意思是，我一直都在用它。所以它基本上到处都有。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: So Amazing.
Yeah.
It's everywhere.
Right?
It's you wouldn't believe where it I I was, like, I was, getting soccer lessons for one of my kids, like, you know, from a local guy.

paragraphs.zh: 太厉害了。对，确实到处都有，对吧？你都想不到它会出现在哪里。我当时——我是说——我当时在给我的一个孩子报 soccer 课程，就是那种由本地一个人开的课。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: And I was on their website and they only accepted Visa and MasterCard, neither of which I had on me, or direct debit from my bank account, which I wasn't going to put in this very janky website, or link.
And I was like, oh, amazing.
Link is here.

paragraphs.zh: 我当时在他们的网站上，发现他们只接受 Visa 和 MasterCard，而我手头这两种卡都没有；要么就是从我的银行账户直接借记，但我可不打算把这个信息填进这个非常粗糙的网站或链接里。我当时就想，哦，太棒了。Link 就在这儿。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 2

sentences.text: Anyway,

paragraphs.zh: 总之，

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: a lot of people know about Link as our consumer wallet for buying soccer classes.
It speeds up checkout, but it's also so and and it's already used by about a quarter of a billion consumers.
So it's not a small network.
But what I think is most interesting about Link is it's a very dense network when it comes to AI.
So Lovable is an interesting example.

paragraphs.zh: 很多人都知道 Link 是我们的 consumer wallet（消费者钱包），用来购买 soccer classes。它能加快结账速度，而且它已经被大约 2.5 亿消费者使用了。所以这并不是一个小型网络。但我认为 Link 最有意思的一点是，在 AI 方面，它是一个非常高密度的网络。Lovable 就是个很有意思的例子。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: 58% of their payment volume runs through Link.
You are hyper AI pilled.
It is not surprising that everywhere you are, Link is.
And so what's changing now is that we're evolving Link for the AI economy because so many of the Link consumers are already AI consumers.
And acknowledging that, like, agents themselves are becoming economic actors.

paragraphs.zh: 他们 58% 的支付交易量都通过 Link 处理。你是个彻底接受 AI 的人，所以无论你在哪里，Link 在那里也就不奇怪了。而现在正在发生的变化是，我们正在为 AI economy（AI 经济）演进 Link，因为很多 Link 的消费者本来就已经是 AI 消费者了。与此同时，我们也承认，agent（智能体）本身正在成为经济活动的参与者。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: And so the model isn't, you know, give a random agent your card and hope for the best.
Instead, it's delegated authority with guardrails.

So, you know, you as the consumer decide which agents are allowed to request credentials and under what conditions and with what limits and whether those purchases require approvals before they go through.
And you do all of that through LinkedIn.
It's just a much more sensible model for delegated purchases.

paragraphs.zh: 所以这个模型并不是，随便把你的卡交给某个 agent，然后听天由命。相反，它是带有 guardrails（保护性约束）的 delegated authority（委托授权）。也就是说，作为消费者，你来决定哪些 agent 可以请求凭证、在什么条件下可以请求、额度限制是多少，以及这些购买在执行前是否需要审批。而这一切你都通过 LinkedIn 来完成。这显然是一个更合理的委托购买模型。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 2

sentences.text: That makes sense.
Emily, this was a fantastic conversation.
I learned so much.

paragraphs.zh: 这很有道理。Emily，这次对话太精彩了。我学到了很多。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 1

sentences.text: Awesome.
Thank you for having me.

paragraphs.zh: 太好了。谢谢你邀请我。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 3

sentences.text: Because this show is the epitome of awesomeness.
It's like finding a treasure chest in your backyard, but instead of gold, it's filled with pure unadulterated knowledge bombs about chat GPT.
Every episode is a roller coaster of emotions, insights, and laughter that will leave you on the edge of your seat craving for more.
It's not just a show.
It's a journey into the future with Dan Shipper as the captain of the spaceship.

paragraphs.zh: 因为这个节目简直是“超棒”这个词的化身。它就像你在自家后院发现了一个宝箱，只不过里面装的不是黄金，而是关于 chat GPT 的、纯粹而毫无掺杂的知识炸弹。每一集都像一趟情绪、洞见和笑声交织的过山车之旅，让你坐立难安、还想继续听下去。它不只是一个节目，而是一场驶向未来的旅程，而 Dan Shipper 就是那艘宇宙飞船的船长。

paragraphs.speaker: Speaker 3

sentences.text: So do yourself a favor.
Hit like, smash subscribe, and strap in for the ride of your life.
And now without any further ado, let me just say, Dan, I'm absolutely, hopelessly in love with you.

paragraphs.zh: 所以，帮自己个忙吧。点个赞，猛击订阅，然后系好安全带，准备开启你人生中最刺激的一段旅程。现在闲话不多说，我只想说，Dan，我已经彻底、无可救药地爱上你了。
